symptoms.symptom_name: Profound new symmetrical generalised weakness and loss of ADL independence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid symmetrical weakness after prolonged bed rest is the core feature of deconditioning.
Symmetry and bilateral distribution argue against a focal cerebrovascular cause, but exclusion needs examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dizziness / near-syncope on standing that settles on sitting

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Postural symptom timing is characteristic of orthostatic hypotension; the objective drop still needs measurement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent 14-day pneumonia admission with prolonged bed rest

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Immobility during acute illness is the direct cause of deconditioning and disuse sarcopenia.
Recent immobility and hospitalisation are strong VTE risk factors mandating a screen.
A recent antihypertensive dose increase is a common iatrogenic contributor to postural hypotension in the older person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced oral intake, likely weight loss and mild new cognitive slowing

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Poor intake predisposes to volume depletion and AKI, worsening postural hypotension; needs bloods.
Constitutional decline and new cognitive slowing can be an atypical presentation of infection in the frail elder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-falls reaching the bathroom in an unsafe home environment

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Falls-risk from impaired mobility and postural drop; drives urgency and home-safety action.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with no productive cough, comfortable at rest (carer-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Carer-reported absence of fever/cough lowers but does not exclude infection in a frail elder; a home thermometer is unverified.

symptoms.symptom_name: No unilateral calf swelling, redness, warmth or tenderness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bilateral symmetry without focal calf signs makes DVT less likely, though it does not fully exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No facial droop, slurred speech, unilateral limb weakness or chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of carer-observable focal signs lowers stroke probability; a formal neuro exam is still needed to exclude.

symptoms.symptom_name: Postural BP drop ~15 mmHg systolic on standing with symptomatic dizziness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Measured lying-and-standing BP drop with symptoms is diagnostic — a clinician measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical proximal muscle wasting (quadriceps/glutei) with reduced grip strength and an unsteady wide-based gait

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Disuse sarcopenia and gait instability confirm severity — a hands-on examination the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bloods (FBC, U&E/creatinine/eGFR, CRP) to exclude occult infection and AKI

investigation_details.expected_result: Ordered via the regular GP to confirm metabolic stability and exclude infection/AKI; results not available in real time via telehealth.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A reversible occult infection or acute kidney injury could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bloods are needed to exclude reversible serious causes; the AI cannot obtain them and must route to in-person care.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms symptomatic orthostatic hypotension and guides medication review; must be done in person.
Excludes occult infection and acute kidney injury in a frail elder with acute change and reduced intake.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Multidomain in-person falls-risk and functional (gait/balance/strength) assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantifies mobility and falls risk and directs rehabilitation and home-safety measures the AI cannot assess remotely.
